differentials.name: acute pain from torsion / hemorrhage; menstrual irregularity
differentials.raw: acute pain from torsion / hemorrhage; menstrual irregularity

differentials.name: chronic pain ← slowly enlarging mass, impaction, adhesions
differentials.raw: chronic pain ← slowly enlarging mass, impaction, adhesions

context: Radiologic guidelines: ◊  Imaging features of ovarian neoplasms virtually never allow a specific diagnosis. Regardless of further differentiation patients always undergo surgery! Signs suggestive of benignancy: √  unilocularity of cyst √  thin wall < 3 mm √  minimal septations √  absence of papillary projection Signs suggestive of malignancy: √  solid nonfatty nonfibrous tissue (most POWERFUL PREDICTOR of malignancy!) √  many solid-tissue elements in a complex lesion √  wall thickness > 3 mm √  inner wall irregularities / papillary projections √  thick septations > 3 mm √  increased echogenici
